50,000 in the first year.\u003c/p\u003e\u003ch2\u003eRisk Management for Prop Firms\u003c/h2\u003e\u003cp\u003eThe primary risk is a cluster of successful traders simultaneously holding large positions. Manage aggregate exposure by setting limits on total position volume. Use hedging through a liquidity provider to neutralize directional risk when aggregate exposure exceeds thresholds.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cimg src=\"/assets/blog/launch-prop-trading-company-guide.jpg\" alt=\"Prop trading risk management\" style=\"width:100%;border-radius:8px;margin:1.5rem 0\" data-post-slug=\"launch-prop-trading-company-guide\" data-image-kind=\"content\" srcset=\"/assets/blog/launch-prop-trading-company-guide-480.jpg 480w, /assets/blog/launch-prop-trading-company-guide-768.jpg 768w, /assets/blog/launch-prop-trading-company-guide.jpg 1280w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 767px) calc(100vw - 2rem), 800px\" width=\"1280\" height=\"720\" /\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://maketrades.info\"\u003eMakeTrades\u003c/a\u003e offers a turnkey solution for launching prop trading companies with integrated risk management.\u003c/p\u003e","image_url":"/assets/blog/launch-prop-trading-company-guide.jpg","language":"en","published":true,"hidden_from_users":true,"created_at":"2026-01-25T19:06:43.907602+00:00","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:06:43.907602+00:00","author":"MakeTrades Team","category":"Prop Trading","tags":["prop company","launch","business model","challenge","risk management"],"reading_time":8,"meta_title":"How to Launch a Prop Trading Company","meta_description":"Guide to starting a prop trading firm. Business model and risk management.","legacy_slug":"launch-prop-trading-company-guide","canonical_slug":"launch-prop-trading-company-guide","alternates":[{"language":"en","slug":"launch-prop-trading-company-guide","legacy_slug":"launch-prop-trading-company-guide"}]};

Back to blog
Prop Trading • 1/25/2026 • 8 min read

How to Launch a Prop Trading Company

Complete guide to starting a prop trading firm: business model, technology stack, legal structure, and marketing.

MakeTrades Team
MakeTrades Expert

The Prop Trading Business Model

Revenue comes from two sources: challenge fees (primary) and profit share from successful traders (secondary). Statistics show 85-90% of traders fail the challenge — their fees cover payouts and operating expenses. Average challenge fee ranges from $200-$500 depending on funded account size.

To launch, you need: a trading platform with challenge module, a legal entity (offshore works since prop trading does not require a broker license in most jurisdictions), payment processing, and marketing budget. Minimum investment: $50,000-$150,000 in the first year.

Risk Management for Prop Firms

The primary risk is a cluster of successful traders simultaneously holding large positions. Manage aggregate exposure by setting limits on total position volume. Use hedging through a liquidity provider to neutralize directional risk when aggregate exposure exceeds thresholds.

MakeTrades offers a turnkey solution for launching prop trading companies with integrated risk management.

Tags: prop companylaunchbusiness modelchallengerisk management
